overview.summary: High-performance server-authoritative projectile system for Unity NGO. Rust owns simulation and collision. C# owns damage, visuals, networking, and game logic. Pattern library is C#-extensible. Movement types live in Rust but are configured from C# per-config.

Burst: parallel struct fill for 8+ simultaneous spawns only

simulation_modes.LocalOnly: Offline/practice. Rust sim, no NGO. LocalProjectileManager owns everything.
simulation_modes.RustSim2D: Server runs Rust 2D tick. Clients predict with origin+dir*speed*t. Snapshots reconcile.
simulation_modes.RustSim3D: Server runs Rust 3D tick. Identical architecture to 2D.
simulation_modes.Raycast: WEAPON script owns Physics2D.Raycast. System handles visual + RPC only.
simulation_modes.PhysicsObject: Unity Rigidbody2D/3D via ObjectNetSync. Low spawn rate only (rockets, grenades).

NativeProjectile_3D.vs_2D: adds Z components + scale_z. timer_t replaces curve_t. angle_deg removed (C# derives from velocity).

movement_types.note: Shooting mode (burst/auto/single/shotgun) is a WEAPON property. Movement type is a physics property assigned per projectile config.

Straight.description: Constant velocity + optional linear accel. Most performant. Default for all guns.

Arching.description: Same as straight but timer_t advances. Use GravityScale for parabolic arc. Arrows, mortars.

Guided.description: Turns toward ax/ay(/az) each tick. C# writes target direction via TickDispatcher Tick_0_1. Homing missiles.

Teleport.description: Discrete jumps every 0.12s. Not interpolated. Blink/phase projectiles.

Wave.description: Sinusoidal lateral oscillation. Params stored Rust-side via register_wave_params(). ax/ay = perp axis set at spawn.

Circular.description: Helical orbit around travel axis. Params stored Rust-side via register_circular_params(). ax/ay(/az) = first perp axis set at spawn.

extensibility.adding_new_type: Add byte constant in simulation.rs match arm. Add tick fn. Update C# ProjectileMovementType enum. CI builds new native lib automatically.
extensibility.without_new_type: For sine/spiral/custom paths: use Straight + C# TickDispatcher subscriber writing ax/ay each Tick_0_1. No Rust changes needed.

wave_circular_config_store.why: These movement types need per-config constants (amplitude, frequency, radius, angular_speed) read every tick inside the hot loop. Storing per-projectile adds 8-16 bytes to ALL projectiles, bloating cache lines for 95% that don't use these types.
wave_circular_config_store.how: Rust-side HashMap<u16, WaveParams> and HashMap<u16, CircularParams>. Registered once at startup via ProjectileLib.register_wave_params() / register_circular_params().
wave_circular_config_store.registration: ProjectileConfigSO.RegisterMovementParams() is called automatically by ProjectileRegistry.Register(). No manual call needed.
wave_circular_config_store.not_a_general_store: ONLY wave and circular params are stored Rust-side. All other config data stays C# only.

damage_system.model: AnimationCurve over normalised travel distance (0 = spawn, 1 = MaxRange). Flat curve = constant damage, zero evaluation overhead.

fields.CritChance: float 0-1 — probability of crit per projectile
fields.CritMultiplier: float — damage multiplier when crit rolls

damage_system.crit_timing: Pre-rolled per projectile at spawn time by ServerProjectileAuthority. Stored as isCrit bool in ServerProjectileData. Applied in RustSimAdapter.ComputeDamage().
damage_system.no_min_max: Min/max random damage is replaced by AnimationCurve. If curve is flat (all keys same value), IsDamageConstant() returns true and callers can skip Evaluate().
damage_system.distance_source: HitResult.TravelDist comes from Rust (NativeProjectile.travel_dist accumulated each tick). Never computed in C#.

ProjId.online: Monotonically increasing counter in ServerProjectileAuthority. Allocated via AllocateProjIds(count).
ProjId.offline: Same counter in LocalProjectileManager. Process-local only.

OwnerMidId.players: Equals NGO OwnerClientId for human players
OwnerMidId.bots: Stable 100-999 range ID assigned at bot spawn. NOT the server's NGO ID (0).
OwnerMidId.why: If bots used OwnerClientId (0 = server), kill attribution and damage type resolution would incorrectly route through server-player logic.

ConfigId.assigned_by: ProjectileRegistry at registration. Session-stable. Not persistent across sessions.

pattern_system.architecture: C#-only. No Rust involvement. Weapon fires BatchSpawnHelper with pre-computed SpawnPoint[].

fields: ControlPoints: Vector2[] in local weapon space (X=horizontal deg, Y=vertical deg)
ProjectileCount: how many bullets sample the spline
SpeedVariance: ±variance per pellet, deterministic with RngSeed
ProjectilePatternSO.editor: ProjectilePatternEditor.cs — interactive spline viewport, drag points, simulate gizmo in scene view

pattern_system.adding_custom_patterns: Add a static method to BatchSpawnHelper or a companion PatternLibrary class. Compute SpawnPoint[], pass to BatchSpawnHelper.SpawnBatch2D/3D(). No Rust changes needed.

config_extensibility.core_package_config: ProjectileConfigSO.cs — lean, game-agnostic. Contains only: simulation routing, movement, piercing, scale growth, damage curve, visual, trail, impact.

game_specific_extension.pattern: Derive from ProjectileConfigSO in your game assembly.
game_specific_extension.example: public class MID_ProjectileConfig : ProjectileConfigSO { public LegendaryTier tier; public AudioClip flightSound; }
game_specific_extension.note: All package systems (BatchSpawnHelper, TrailObjectPool, ProjectileRegistry) reference ProjectileConfigSO only — derived class slots in transparently.

client_prediction.model: Deterministic extrapolation: position = origin + direction * speed * elapsed. Matches Rust Straight tick exactly.
client_prediction.history_buffer: CircularBuffer<ProjectileStatePayload> per projectile. Stores predicted position at each server tick.
client_prediction.accuracy: Exact for Straight. Approximate for Arching/Guided — snapshots correct drift.

reconciliation.threshold: If server snapshot differs from predicted by > reconcileThreshold (0.5m default) → smooth lerp over reconcileDuration (0.15s).
reconciliation.hard_snap: If error > hardSnapThreshold (3m default) → instant position snap + origin correction.
reconciliation.snapshot_rate: Every 3-5 FixedUpdates (not every frame). Coarse correction only.

latency_compensation.method: Server computes RTT from clientFireTick vs serverTick. Offsets spawn position by dir * speed * latency. Applied once at spawn.

what_is_synced: SpawnConfirmedClientRpc: configId, origin, dir, speed, baseId, serverTick
HitConfirmedClientRpc: projId, targetId, damage, hitPos, isHeadshot, isCrit
SnapshotClientRpc: (projId, x, y[, z]) array every N ticks
what_is_NOT_synced: Projectile position every frame (no NetworkTransform on bullets)
Damage values in transit (computed server-side only)

rust_updated.changes: Added NativeProjectile3D(84B), HitResult3D(28B), CollisionTarget3D(24B). Added tick_projectiles_3d, check_hits_grid_3d, spawn_batch, spawn_batch_3d, register_wave_params, register_circular_params, movement type constant exports, compile-time size assertions.

Added MOVE_WAVE(4) and MOVE_CIRCULAR(5) constants. Added tick_wave, tick_circular for 2D. Added full 3D tick: tick_all_3d, tick_straight_3d, tick_arching_3d, tick_guided_3d, tick_teleport_3d, tick_wave_3d, tick_circular_3d, tick_scale_3d.
Added CellGrid3D (512 buckets, u64 keys). Added check_hits_3d with 3D sphere-sphere narrow phase. 2D collision unchanged.
NEW FILE. Rust-side HashMap stores for WaveParams and CircularParams indexed by config_id u16. RwLock for thread safety. get_wave/get_circular called from simulation hot loop.

csharp_updated.changes: Added 3D P/Invoke bindings (tick_3d, collision_3d, spawn_batch, spawn_batch_3d, movement param registration). Added ValidateStructSizes() covering all 7 structs.
Added SyncToSimulation(NativeProjectile3D[], int) overload. Extracted RetireExpiredFades(), FindSlot(). ApplyConfig updated for new TrailConfig fields (MinVertexDistance, CapVertices).
Minor: Render() now called by LocalProjectileManager.LateUpdate() and can receive buffer from authority client-side. No rendering logic changes.

deprecated_when_migrated: ConsolidatedProjectileDamageHandler.cs → damage logic absorbed into RustSimAdapter
kept_unchanged: ObjectNetSync.cs            (kept for slow physics objects only)
ProjectileConfigManager.cs  (game-side O(1) cache by enum — unchanged)

step_2_registry.description: Assign all ProjectileConfigSO assets to ProjectileRegistry._autoRegister in the inspector. They will be registered automatically on Awake with stable configIds.

step_3_config_creation.description: Create ProjectileConfigSO assets via Assets > Create > MidMan > Projectile System > Projectile Config.

key_fields.MovementType: Straight for most weapons. Wave/Circular auto-register Rust params.
key_fields.DamageCurve: AnimationCurve x=distance y=damage. Flat = constant damage.
key_fields.UseScaleGrowth: Only enable if projectile grows after spawn. Default false = zero Rust cost.
key_fields.HasTrail: Enable + assign TrailMaterial for trail renderer.
key_fields.PreferredSimMode: Override routing for special cases (e.g. force PhysicsObject).

step_3_config_creation.game_specific_extension: Derive from ProjectileConfigSO for game-specific fields (audio, legendary tier, kill effects, etc.).

step_4_pattern_creation.description: Create ProjectilePatternSO via Assets > Create > MidMan > Projectile System > Projectile Pattern.
step_4_pattern_creation.editor: Select the asset to open the interactive pattern editor. Drag control points to define shot spread shape. Preview button shows rays in Scene view.
step_4_pattern_creation.usage_in_weapon: Call pattern.SampleDirections() to get Vector2[] angles. Convert to world-space SpawnPoint[] at the barrel. Pass to MID_MasterProjectileSystem.Fire().

step_5_weapon_script.description: Weapon scripts call MID_MasterProjectileSystem. They own shooting mode logic (burst, auto, single). The system owns simulation.
rust_sim_example: spawnPts[i] = new SpawnPoint { Origin = barrelTip.position, Direction = dir, Speed = 0f };
raycast_example: HitPoint = hit ? hit.point : barrelTip.position + barrelTip.right * config.MaxRange,

step_6_target_registration.description: Damageable characters register themselves as collision targets.
online: // In character FixedUpdate or TickDispatcher Tick_0_05:
// Game damage system subscribes to RustSimAdapter:

step_8_guided_projectiles.description: For homing/guided projectiles, update the target direction via TickDispatcher.

step_9_impact_effects.description: Register per-config impact strategies for non-default effects.

key_rules: scale_speed = 0 (default) → tick_scale skipped entirely per projectile. Zero cost for non-growing bullets.
Wave/Circular params stored Rust-side → zero per-projectile struct overhead for straight/guided/teleport bullets.
spawn_batch: always one FFI call regardless of pellet count. BatchSpawnHelper uses Burst for 8+ pellets.
TrailObjectPool: applies config only on change. MinVertexDistance 0.1+ for fast projectiles.
TickDispatcher Tick_0_1: use for damage reduction over distance, guided direction updates, trail cleanup, performance monitoring.
Never use NetworkTransform or ObjectNetSync on Rust-sim projectiles.
Snapshot rate 3-5 ticks (not every frame). Snapshot is coarse error correction, not primary position sync.

common_mistakes.fix: Use RegisterRaycastFire() instead. The weapon script owns Physics2D.Raycast().
Rust owns position. Write ax/ay for acceleration only. Never write x/y directly.
Use MID ID (100-999 range). isBotOwner flag disambiguates. See ServerProjectileData.
Derive from ProjectileConfigSO in your game assembly. Package systems use base class only.

common_mistakes.mistake: Registering configs after the first Fire() call
common_mistakes.fix: Register all configs in Awake() or before the first frame. ConfigIds must be stable.
ObjectNetSync is ONLY for slow physics objects (rockets/grenades). Never on Rust-sim bullets.

common_mistakes.mistake: Subscribing to TickDispatcher Tick_0_01 for logic
common_mistakes.fix: Use Tick_0_1 minimum. Tick_0_01 has negative CPU saving below ~100fps. See MID_TickDispatcher docs.
